verdict_summary: BA spec 20260524-101700-D contains two blocking errors: (1) BA asserts that test_AC4 still needs the negative ..HEAD assertion added by dev, but test_AC4 ALREADY has this assertion committed and on disk (82 lines, confirmed by git show HEAD). Dev does NOT need to strengthen test_AC4. (2) BA is correct that test_AC3 lacks the 'files_created_provenance_violation' assertion — M2/AC2 remains valid work. Additionally, AC1's pattern check requires the literal string 'None' in test_AC4, but the already-committed test uses a loop-based assertion that does not contain that literal — creating a spec_text_vs_execution_drift on AC1 itself. BA must correct these errors before dev proceeds.

ba_validation_objections.claim: BA claims 'Dev must strengthen test_AC4 with ..HEAD negative assertion' and frames this as work still to be done
ba_validation_objections.evidence_gap: BA's context JSON field 'diagnosis_completeness' explicitly states 'incomplete (inferred only — test file contents not read in this session)'. BA did NOT read test_AC4 before writing the spec. QA read test_AC4 directly: the file is 82 lines, already contains the full negative ..HEAD assertion (per-line loop using re.search, lines 57-82), and was committed in that form in git commit 20a45af (2026-05-24 11:51:48 UTC). The file mtime is 2026-05-24 10:01:56, before the BA spec was written at 10:17:00. pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/ runs 5/5 PASS including test_AC4 with the negative assertion present and functioning.

verification.method: Read test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py directly; run git show HEAD:tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py | wc -l (returns 82); run pytest (5/5 pass); git log --oneline -- tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py
verification.result: test_AC4 already has the negative assertion. Grep confirms: re.search (line 59, 60, 63, 74), HEAD (lines 39-82 multiple occurrences), ..HEAD warning assertion (line 74-82). The assertion uses a per-line loop approach instead of a single re.search...is None pattern, but achieves the same result and is more precise (exempts explanatory negation text from false positive).
verification.conclusion: BA incorrectly states M1 is work to be done. M1 is ALREADY DONE.

ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA must read test_AC4 before specifying dev tasks. BA should either: (a) drop M1 and AC1 from the spec (since test_AC4 is already updated and passing), OR (b) rewrite M1 to confirm the existing implementation is adequate and mark this as 'confirm only — no dev change needed'. The current spec is sending dev to re-implement something that already exists, risking a regression.

ba_validation_objections.claim: BA's AC1 check requires the literal string 'None' in test_AC4's body as a pattern_present check
ba_validation_objections.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260524-101700-D.json AC1.check.patterns_present = ['diff --name-only', 're.search', 'HEAD', 'None']. The on-disk test_AC4 (already committed, 82 lines) does NOT contain the literal string 'None' — it uses a boolean variable 'is_negation_explanation' in a loop, asserting the explanation is truthy. Running: grep -n 'None' tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py → 0 matches. The AC1 pattern check 'None' would FAIL against the already-correct implementation.

patterns_present_check.HEAD: PRESENT (multiple lines)
patterns_present_check.None: MISSING — test uses loop with assert is_negation_explanation, not assert re.search(...) is None

verification.conclusion: AC1's pattern check 'None' is spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the AC specifies 'asserting the result is None' but the existing (and correct) implementation uses a different assertion form. If dev were to 'update test_AC4' to satisfy AC1's 'None' pattern, they would likely replace the existing superior implementation with a simpler one that introduces false positives on explanatory text.

ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA must update AC1.check.patterns_present to match what the existing test actually contains. Replace 'None' with 'is_negation_explanation' (the actual boolean variable used). Or, since test_AC4 is already correct and passing, remove AC1 entirely and trust the existing implementation.

ba_validation_objections.claim: BA did not verify when spec M5 text was last modified or whether the QA failure referenced a now-obsolete version of the spec
ba_validation_objections.evidence: The original QA failure (qa-report-20260522-080646-D.json, iteration 1) references 'ticket-20260522-080646-D.md:84' with text 'at commit time, HEAD is updated'. This text does NOT exist in the current ticket file. The ticket file was CREATED on 2026-05-24 10:18 (birth = 10:18:54). The iter1 QA report was modified on 2026-05-23 (mtime = 15:45:50 on May 23). This is chronologically impossible: the QA ran and found text in a file that did not yet exist. The resolution is that the QA iter1 ran against a working-tree version of the spec before it was corrected and committed. BA-QA iter3 (for the 20260522-080646-D cycle) confirmed 'Finding 1 (M5 ..HEAD) is RESOLVED'. BA-QA report ba-qa-report-20260522-080646-D-iter3.json status=pass. BA for task 20260524-101700-D did not read the iter3 BA-QA report to verify that M5 and test_AC4 were already resolved in the prior cycle.

verification.iter3_ba_qa_status: pass — both iter2 blocking findings resolved per ba-qa-report-20260522-080646-D-iter3.json
verification.ticket_file_birth: 2026-05-24 10:18:54 — file created AFTER iter1 QA ran (May 23)
verification.current_m5_text: Lines 87-90 contain correct working-tree form with ..HEAD WRONG annotation — verified by direct file read AND git show 20a45af (original commit content identical to current)
verification.conclusion: BA failed to check whether the prior BA QA cycle already resolved the M5 issue. The current task was created to fix something already fixed. At minimum, M1 and the M5-related portions of this spec are unnecessary.

ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA must read ba-qa-report-20260522-080646-D-iter3.json BEFORE writing a remediation spec. The iter3 report would have confirmed M5 and test_AC4 are resolved. This would have narrowed the current task to only M2/AC2 (test_AC3 files_created assertion) which is the one remaining genuine gap.

ba_validation_objections.claim: BA's scope includes M1 (test_AC4 update) which is already done, inflating dev scope unnecessarily
ba_validation_objections.evidence: The only genuine remaining gap is: test_AC3 lacks the 'files_created_provenance_violation' assertion. Confirmed: grep -n 'files_created_provenance_violation' test_AC3_2059b2b3bcc430f6.py → 0 matches. The test has 3 assertions (dev_provenance_violation, baseline_head_sha, primary_cause.*dev_implementation) but not the files_created label. BA is correct on this point. However, the scope is inflated by including M1 (already done) as a dev task.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA should reduce scope to: M1 becomes 'Confirm test_AC4 is adequate (no dev change needed)'; M2 remains 'Update test_AC3 to assert files_created_provenance_violation in qa.md'; only AC2 and AC3 remain as dev tasks; AC1 is either dropped or rewritten as a confirmation check.

ba_validation_objections.claim: BA identifies both test files as requiring dev changes
ba_validation_objections.evidence: test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py: ALREADY UPDATED, no dev change needed. test_AC3_2059b2b3bcc430f6.py: genuinely needs update (missing files_created_provenance_violation assertion). BA's identification of test_AC3 is accurate; identification of test_AC4 as needing changes is inaccurate.

verification.test_AC4_status: 82 lines, negative assertion present, 5/5 pytest pass — no change needed
verification.test_AC3_status: 31 lines, missing 'files_created_provenance_violation' — dev change genuinely needed

ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: Remove test_AC4 from files_to_modify in development_approach. Keep test_AC3.

evidence_quality.rationale: BA's context JSON explicitly states diagnosis_completeness='incomplete (inferred only — test file contents not read in this session)'. BA drew conclusions about what test_AC4 contains without reading the file. This produced the central error in the spec: claiming test_AC4 needs the negative assertion when it already has it. Per evidence_quality dimension, every factual claim must be backed by investigation proof; 'BA inferred' is not evidence.

scope_alignment.rationale: The user requirement is to fix the remaining QA-identified gaps from the 20260522-080646-D cycle. BA-QA iter3 already confirmed M5 and test_AC4 are resolved. Including M1 (test_AC4 update) as a dev task misaligns with the actual remaining scope. Only test_AC3 needs dev work. The inflated scope risks regressing test_AC4 if dev replaces the existing correct implementation.

investigation_completeness.rationale: BA did not read: (1) the prior BA-QA iter3 report that already resolved M5 and test_AC4, (2) the actual contents of test_AC4. These two reads would have narrowed the spec to a single file change (test_AC3). BA relied on the iter1 QA failure description without checking whether subsequent iterations had already resolved it.

affected_file_accuracy.rationale: test_AC3 identification is accurate (correct file, correct gap). test_AC4 identification as 'needs changes' is inaccurate (already updated). The file paths exist and the BA's description of what test_AC3 lacks is correct. Half-credit on this dimension.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.rationale: AC1.check.patterns_present includes 'None' which is not present in the already-committed test_AC4. The AC says the test should 'assert result is None' but the implementation uses a boolean loop variable. This is a drift between the AC literal text and the runnable test. BA must update AC1 to match the actual implementation form, or remove AC1 since the test is already correct.

m5_spec_text.evidence: ticket-20260522-080646-D.md lines 87-90 read directly: 'Phase 2 runs before staging and commit — ..HEAD form is WRONG here and returns empty set'. Also confirmed by git show 20a45af:docs/dev/ticket-20260522-080646-D.md which shows the file was FIRST committed with this correct text.

test_AC4_negative_assertion.evidence: test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py is 82 lines, committed in git HEAD at 20a45af, contains per-line loop with re.search for ..HEAD detection (lines 57-82), pytest 5/5 pass. File mtime 2026-05-24 10:01:56 — before BA spec was written at 10:17:00.

test_AC3_files_created_assertion.evidence: test_AC3_2059b2b3bcc430f6.py is 31 lines with 3 assertions. grep 'files_created_provenance_violation' returns 0 matches. This is the only genuine remaining dev work.

agents_qa_md_files_created_check.evidence: agents/qa.md lines 425-438 directly read: git ls-files --others --exclude-standard check with label 'files_created_provenance_violation' present.

prior_cycle_resolution.claim: M5 and test_AC4 resolved in prior cycle
prior_cycle_resolution.evidence: ba-qa-report-20260522-080646-D-iter3.json status=pass, iter3 found 'both iter2 blocking findings resolved'. The current task duplicates already-resolved work.

recommended_spec_corrections.correction: Remove reference to test_AC4 needing updates. State only: 'Strengthen test_AC3 to assert files_created_provenance_violation is present in agents/qa.md.'

Replace with: 'CONFIRM that tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py already contains the negative ..HEAD assertion (no dev change needed). Current implementation uses per-line loop with re.search — this is correct and more precise than a single-pattern is None check.'

Either drop AC1 (test_AC4 already correct), or rewrite as: 'GIVEN test_AC4 on disk WHEN pytest is run THEN test passes and grep finds re.search and ..HEAD and is_negation_explanation in the file body'. Remove 'None' from patterns_present as it is not present in the correct implementation.
Keep as-is. test_AC3 genuinely lacks files_created_provenance_violation assertion. Dev should add: assert 'files_created_provenance_violation' in text to test_AC3.
Remove test_AC4_7c792480bc275ff9.py from files_to_modify. Only test_AC3_2059b2b3bcc430f6.py needs modification.
